To what extent does H&M response to labor abuse allegations

To what extent does H&M response to labor abuse allegations

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Repeatedly links ethical dimension of sustainability to H&Mâs supply-chain practices
Sound use of living-wage pilots, contract insecurity and harassment data to illustrate labour issues
weaknesses: Integration is unevenâsome sections drift into narrative without explicit conceptual analysis
Occasional over-reliance on industry context rather than organization-specific analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 is justified because the student demonstrates clear understanding of the sustainability/ethics concept and connects it analytically to H&Mâs practices, but integration is not fully sustained throughout the commentary, preventing a higher score.

strengths: Provides four distinct supporting documents, meeting the 3â5 requirement
Documents are relevant and include investigative reports, H&M market data and a motivation theory article
weaknesses: Depth of sources variesâsome (e.g., popular psychology site) lack academic rigor
Range of ideas could be broader to reach in-depth, varied perspectives
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 reflects that the student supplies an appropriate number of relevant documents with adequate depth, but the range and academic depth fall short of the highest band.

strengths: Selection of Maslowâs hierarchy and financial ratios shows basic use of theory and tools
Application links Maslow to worker needs and financial data to H&Mâs capacity for wage increases
weaknesses: Tool selection is narrowâadditional frameworks (CSR pyramid, stakeholder mapping) are missing
Application occasionally descriptive and not always tightly tied to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 2 is appropriate because while the student selects and applies some relevant tools and theories, the range and depth of selection are limited and application is only adequately, rather than effectively, connected to the research question.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses both quantitative (factory counts, net sales, harassment percentages) and qualitative (union testimony) data
Analysis links contract insecurity to Maslowâs safety needs and reputational risk to H&Mâs strategy
weaknesses: Integration of data and tools fluctuates, with some descriptive passages
Lacks deeper comparative benchmarks or sustained sophisticated analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 reflects adequate selection and use of data and mostly effective analysis that goes beyond description, though the depth and sustained integration needed for the top bands are not consistently present.

strengths: Conclusions align broadly with evidence and revisit the research question
Provides concrete recommendations (intensified audits, transparent wage data)
weaknesses: Some recommendations lack feasibility analysis (e.g., global living-wage adoption)
Partial development of stakeholder and financial implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 2 is warranted because conclusions are mostly consistent with evidence and explicitly answer the question, but reasoning is not fully developed across all dimensions.

strengths: Follows IB structure with clear headings and logical flow
Includes all required sections in the prescribed order
weaknesses: Some digressions disrupt flow and document headers are misaligned in places
Developer markup and artifacts remain in appendix
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 2 applies as the organization and structure are appropriate throughout, despite some formatting inconsistencies and digressions.

strengths: All required presentation elements are included (title page, word count, references, appendices)
Minor inconsistencies do not compromise the presence of key elements
weaknesses: Citation formatting and in-text markers are inconsistent
Unnecessary artifacts (advertising markers, blank pages) detract from professionalism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 2 is justified because all required elements are present and correctly formatted at a basic level, though there are multiple areas for polishing to achieve polish and consistency.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows the prescribed IB structure (title, executive summary, introduction, analysis, conclusion, references).  Headings and sub-headings create logical flow, though some sections are lengthy and contain digressions.  Overall, structure is appropriate, meeting the criteria for band 2.

The documents are relevantâtwo investigative reports on H&Mâs labour abuses, H&Mâs own market data, and a motivation theory article used later in employee-needs analysis.  Depth is adequate but some sources (e.g., Maslow article from a popular psychology site) are relatively surface-level, so the range and depth fall short of the âin-depth and range of ideasâ standard for band 4.

All required elementsâtitle page, word count, references, appendices, and correctly formatted supporting documentsâare present.  Minor citation inconsistencies are noted but do not amount to missing elements, justifying placement in the highest presentation band.
Four distinct and citable documents are supplied, fitting the requirement of 3-5 sources.  This matches the descriptors for band 3 where 3-5 documents are correctly presented.

Maslow is applied to argue that unmet physiological and safety needs explain dissatisfaction among supplier-factory workers, and financial ratios are linked to H&Mâs capacity to absorb wage increases.  The application is generally relevant, though at times descriptive and not always tightly tied back to the research question, indicating âadequateâ rather than âeffectiveâ application.

The IA cites quantitative evidence (number of factories, percentage of harassment cases, net sales, profit) and qualitative testimony from unions.  Data selection covers both primary and secondary perspectives, fulfilling an âadequateâ standard.  Usage of figures to support arguments is present but not consistently integrated into deeper calculations or comparative benchmarks, so band 3 is appropriate.

Analysis goes beyond descriptionâlinking contract insecurity to Maslowâs safety needs and discussing how reputational risk might impact omni-channel growthâbut the depth fluctuates and occasionally reiterates source content.  Integration of concept, data and tools is âmostly effectiveâ yet not sustained or sophisticated enough for band 4.

The research questionâhow effectively is H&M ensuring ethical labour practices in its supply chain?âis explicitly revisited in the final section, and an answer is provided, but the reasoning is partially developed and leaves certain aspects (financial implications, stakeholder responses) insufficiently addressed.  This aligns with a âpartial answerâ descriptor.

The student employs Maslowâs hierarchy, refers to living-wage calculations, and cites financial key figures, indicating an adequate selection of motivational and financial tools.  While additional business frameworks (e.g., CSR pyramid, stakeholder mapping) would have strengthened breadth, the selection surpasses a merely âsome toolsâ level and fits band 3.

The conclusion that H&M should intensify supplier audits and transparently publish wage data aligns broadly with the evidence assembled and answers the research question, but some recommendations (e.g., global living-wage adoption) are asserted without full feasibility analysis.  This fits a âsome but not all evidence-consistentâ standard of band 2.

H&M is clearly the focal organisation.  The student analyses specific supplier practices (fixed-duration contracts, pregnancy dismissals, wage theft) and connects them to H&Mâs strategic claims and omni-channel expansion plans.  This demonstrates clear analysis of the link between the concept and the organisation.  Nevertheless, the integration is unevenâfinancial data from the annual report, for instance, is quoted but not always analysed in relation to labour issuesâso the work sits solidly in band 3 rather than 4.

The student repeatedly links the ethical dimension of sustainability/labour rights to H&Mâs supply-chain practices and draws on several sources to explain the gap between public commitments and workplace reality.  References to living-wage pilots, contract insecurity and sexual-harassment data show a sound grasp of the key concept and move beyond mere description.  However, integration is not fully sustained across all parts of the commentary; some sections drift into narrative about the ILO or other brands without explicit conceptual analysis, so the work does not reach the âfully integrated throughoutâ standard of band 5.

annotations.comment: The narrative excerpt is informative but could be condensed. The student might summarize key findings rather than pasting extended text.
The block quote lacks an in-text citation marker. Add citations for quoted statements to maintain academic rigour.
The âMADE IN MYANMARâ section is interesting but drifts from H&M to broader industry context. Focus on organizationâspecific sources.
The clear âSupporting Documentsâ header enhances navigability, establishing a well-organized appendix structure.
The first supporting document citation lacks an accessed date. Include access dates for online sources to improve completeness.
The URL is presented without hyperlink formatting or a retrieval date. Format URLs consistently and record when they were accessed.
Including the full report title twice is redundant. Consolidate citation elements to avoid repetition.
The three list items on page 2 effectively summarise key survey statistics, showing relevant depth.
The bullet on fixed-duration contracts is highly relevant to labourârights analysis. Good linkage to H&Mâs commitments.
The citation âSupporting Document 2â appears after narrative text. Place document headings consistently at the start of each section.
The multi-paragraph description overwhelms the appendix. The student could instead extract key points in their own words.
The Reuters excerpt is timely but includes advertisement markers (âReport this adâ). Remove these artifacts to improve focus.
The passage quoting H&Mâs response lacks a source date. Add dates to all statements of fact or company quotes.
The âSupporting Document 3â header on page 6 follows unrelated content. Begin each new document with its header to avoid confusion.
The H&M group expansion text provides quantitative context but has limited bearing on labour ethics. Consider adding HRâspecific data.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The storeâmarket table is well structured and conveys key figures clearly, supporting quantitative analysis.
The decision to include Inditex and Primark context is useful, but the student should explicitly link these comparisons back to H&Mâs strategy.
The lengthy description of Maslowâs five levels could be summarized more concisely. Focus on levels applied to analysis.
The table lacks a clear title or source line. Add a descriptive title and source citation for transparency.
Footnote marker â*â in the table is appropriate but the explanatory note should be aligned directly under the table for clarity.
Citing Maslowâs theory from a popular psychology site limits academic depth. Replace with peer-reviewed journal sources.
The video metadata (e.g. â0 seconds of 1 minute, 43 secondsâ) is included verbatim. Exclude playback controls and focus on content.
The âSectionHeaderâ labels remain visible in the appendix text. Remove developer markup before final submission.
The blank final page appears to be a formatting artifact. Remove empty pages to streamline presentation.
